channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: The 85-Year-Old Widow Snagged by Trump’s Immigration Crackdown
item.description: Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé, an 85-year-old French widow of a former G. I., was deported from the US under Trump's immigration crackdown. In her first interview since being deported, she recounted her experience in ICE detention.

item.title: Gujarati Woman Shot At US Grocery Store, Husband Arrested For Illegal Gun Use
item.description: Sharad Gajera was arrested for illegal gun use after his wife was shot at a US grocery store. No court date has been set yet for Sharad Gajera. Gujarati Woman Shot At US Grocery Store, Husband Arrested For Illegal Gun Use.

item.title: Europe Prepares for a Longer War in Ukraine, With No Strategy to End It
item.description: Europe prepares for a longer war in Ukraine with no clear path to victory or peace. With American dealmakers wrapped up with Iran, neither Russia nor Ukraine has a clear path to victory.

item.title: US, Iran To Hold 2nd Round Of Talks In Pakistan Today. What's On Agenda?
item.description: Iran and the US will hold a second round of peace talks in Pakistan. The previous round occurred on April 11-12, amid a delicate ceasefire. The previous round of talks was held on April 11-12.

item.title: ‘Astonishing’ discovery could help save children from deadly disfiguring condition
item.description: Researchers found a new bacteria species in noma patients that could lead to better treatments.

item.title: Top Iranian and Pakistani officials meet in Islamabad
item.description: Iran's FM Abbas Araghchi met Pakistani officials in Islamabad for talks. Iran’s FM Abbas Araghchi is in Islamabad for talks with Pakistani officials, as US envoys are also set to arrive.

item.title: Iran executes man over 'mission' for Israel's spy agency
item.description: Iran executed Erfan Kiani for allegedly working with Israel's spy agency. Erfan Kiani was hanged after his sentence was upheld by the country's Supreme Court, the judiciary's Mizan Online website said.

item.title: Overnight Russian attacks on Ukraine kill five, injure 30
item.description: Russia launched over 600 drones and 47 missiles at Ukraine, killing five and injuring 30 in eight regions. Russia launched more than 600 drones and 47 missiles as it targeted eight regions in Dnipro, authorities say.

item.title: Polls open in Gaza area in first municipal election in 20 years
item.description: Polls opened in Gaza's Deir el-Balah for the first municipal election in 20 years. Gaza officials say Deir el-Balah vote is a 'pilot', while occupied West Bank also goes to first polls since 2023 war.

item.title: Iran resumes commercial flights from Tehran’s international airport for first time since conflict
item.description: Iran resumed commercial flights from Tehran's international airport after a two-month halt due to conflict. Iran has resumed commercial flights from Tehran’s international airport for the first time since the conflict with the U. S.

item.title: On Iran’s Border, Cooking Oil Trade Is a Snapshot of the Country’s Struggles
item.description: Iran's economic crisis has forced many to cross into Turkey to buy basic goods like cooking oil. The war has exacerbated Iran’s economic crisis, forcing many to cross the border into Turkey to buy the most basic goods.

item.title: Australia and New Zealand gather in Turkiye to commemorate WWI battle
item.description: Australia and New Zealand commemorated a WWI battle in Turkiye with hymns, prayers, and wreath-laying ceremonies. The hour-long event included mournful hymns, prayers and the laying of wreaths by the participants, which included representatives from many countries around the world.

item.title: 'Panda bond' continues as China sends giant pandas to US zoo
item.description: China is sending two giant pandas to the Atlanta zoo under a 10-year conservation deal. The pandas will be on loan from China for the next decade. Two giant pandas from China are headed to Atlanta on a new 10-year conservation deal.

item.title: OpenAI chief apologizes for not reporting shooting suspect to police
item.description: OpenAI's chief executive has apologized for not reporting a shooting suspect to the police. The executive said the company should have contacted authorities sooner. OpenAI chief apologizes for not reporting shooting suspect to police.

item.title: Major Russian attack on Ukraine kills four, wounds dozens
item.description: Russia launched a major attack on Ukraine, killing four and wounding dozens with drones and missiles. Russia has been launching smaller ⁠barrages of dozens of drones every night at Ukraine, interspersing them ‌with occasional large-scale attacks that use hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles.

item.title: Ireland to demand Israel pay for settler demolition of Palestinian school
item.description: Ireland will demand Israel repay the cost of a demolished Palestinian school. Ireland will demand that Israeli authorities repay the cost of a school that was demolished by settlers. Ireland to demand Israel pay for settler demolition of Palestinian school.

item.title: Iran executes atomic agency worker over ‘spying’ charge, hangs protester as crackdown intensifies
item.description: Iran has executed an atomic agency worker accused of spying and hanged a protester amid a widening crackdown. The executions come as tensions between Iran and the West escalate. Iran executes atomic agency worker over ‘spying’ charge, hangs protester as crackdown intensifies.

item.title: No clarity on U.S.-Iran talks as Islamabad stays under lockdown
item.description: Pakistan's Deputy Prime Minister expressed hope for meaningful engagements between the US and Iran to promote regional peace and stability. Pakistan's Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Dar, in a social media post, expressed hope for "meaningful engagements" between the two warring parties to promote regional peace and stability.

item.title: Romania says drone fragments damage property during overnight Russian attack on Ukraine
item.description: Romania's Defence Ministry condemned Russia's overnight attack on Ukraine, which damaged property with drone fragments, posing a new challenge to regional security. The Defence Ministry firmly condemns the irresponsible actions of the Russian Federation and emphasises that these represent ​a new challenge ‌to regional security and stability in the Black Sea area.

item.title: US says two killed in boat strike as toll climbs over 180
item.description: The US military conducted a strike on a vessel, killing two, with a toll over 180. The military "conducted a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations", the US Southern Command said.
